mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO growth and 60-day forward SPX return remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average return in periods of increasing INDPRO is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 2.2 million units signal robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the economy absorbs excess housing capacity.
failure_condition: In instances where HOUST exceeds 2200, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in less than 55% of cases, or the average return is not statistically different from zero at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 3% and retail sales growth exceeds 1% month-over-month, accommodative monetary policy combines with robust consumer spending to boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the joint trigger condition holds, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not positive at a 5% significance level, or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets (unemployment rate below 4.5%), further declines in the unemployment rate lead to overheating concerns and preemptive Fed tightening, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 4.5%, the mean 20-day forward return following a month-over-month decline in UNRATE is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the return distribution does not have a negative median.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in headline CPI (above 0.3%) raises inflation expectations and triggers fears of monetary tightening, causing a negative short-term S&P 500 return over 5 trading days as investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: When CPI growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in less than 55% of cases, or the average return is not statistically different from zero at 10% significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong industrial production and retail sales indicate broad economic momentum from both supply and demand sides, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings confidence rises.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO > 118 and RSAFS > 350000 are met, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in less than 60% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls consistently signal improving employment conditions and consumer income, which supports equity valuations through higher spending power over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average return in months with positive payroll growth is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (federal funds rate at or below 2.5%), modest increases in the rate signal central bank confidence in the economic recovery, which is absorbed positively by equity markets over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS <= 2.5%, the mean 60-day forward return following a month-over-month increase in FEDFUNDS is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the win rate is below 50%.
